title: Zone Trust Level Verification — SL-T vs Observed Purdue Layer

compliance.assurance_rationale: Uplifted from contributing to partial: UC provides meaningful monitoring evidence for SR 1.1.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that IEC 62443 SR 1.1 (Human user identification and authentication) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.15.55: Zone Trust Level Verification — SL-T vs Observed Purdue Layer.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.15.55' running on ot_assets with sl_t; ot_topology observed layer, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

spl:
index=ot_assets OR index=ot_topology earliest=-1d
| eval id=coalesce(asset_id, device_id)
| stats latest(sl_t) as required_tier, latest(observed_purdue_level) as observed by id
| eval mismatch=case(required_tier="SL-3" AND observed>2,1, required_tier="SL-2" AND observed>3,1, true(),0)
| where mismatch=1
| table id, required_tier, observed
description: Joins SL-T/Criticality from inventory to observed Purdue level to find assets operating below required trust level for their criticality.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for IEC 62443 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes ot_assets, ot_topology. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Refresh topology weekly from IDS; (2) reconcile moves with CMDB; (3) migration plan to correct zone; (4) risk acceptance workflow for exceptions. Indexes required: `index=ot_assets`, `index=ot_topology`.
visualization: Table, Scatter (tier vs level), Single value (count). Key fields: `id`, `mismatch`, `observed`, `required_tier`.

references.title: Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Active Directory

knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Industrial Asset Intelligence.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `ot_assets` with `sl_t`; `ot_topology` observed layer.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Refresh topology weekly from IDS; (2) reconcile moves with CMDB; (3) migration plan to correct zone; (4) risk acceptance workflow for exceptions.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=ot_assets OR index=ot_topology earliest=-1d
| eval id=coalesce(asset_id, device_id)
| stats latest(sl_t) as required_tier, latest(observed_purdue_level) as observed by id
| eval mismatch=case(required_tier="SL-3" AND observed>2,1, required_tier="SL-2" AND observed>3,1, true(),0)
| where mismatch=1
| table id, required_tier, observed
```

Understanding this SPL

**Zone Trust Level Verification — SL-T vs Observed Purdue Layer (IEC 62443-3-2 / Zone trust level verification)** — Joins SL-T/Criticality from inventory to observed Purdue level to find assets operating below required trust level for their criticality.

Documented **Data sources**: `ot_assets` with `sl_t`; `ot_topology` observed layer. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Industrial Asset Intelligence. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: ot_assets, ot_topology.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=ot_assets, index=ot_topology, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **id** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `stats` aggregates events by **id**, producing one row per unique combination for trend analysis and threshold evaluation.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **mismatch** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Filters the current rows with `where mismatch=1` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Output fields: id, mismatch, observed, required_tier.

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table, Scatter (tier vs level), Single value (count).
grandmaExplanation: We watch for joins sl-t/criticality from inventory to observed purdue level to find assets operating below required trust level for their criticality. This matters because it catches problems that could lead to audit findings or compliance gaps.
